DP.052.0001:
[0103,0000]: OK, gramps!
I brought [0103,0001] like I said!\f
All right, I’ll be outta here!\f
DP.052.0004:
Rowan: Hm! Everyone’s here finally.
[0103,0000], everyone, listen.\f
You may have forgotten already, but
I study the evolution of Pokémon.\f
DP.052.0005:
But the more I study, the more
mysteries appear and multiply.\f
Pokémon that evolve, and those that
don’t...\f
What makes them different from each
other?\f
Do those that are immature as living
beings evolve to ones more mature?\f
If so, what do we make of the
legendary Pokémon that don’t evolve?\f
Are we to assume that the legendary
Pokémon are complete as creatures?\f
DP.052.0006:
This is where you three come in.\f
In the three lakes of Sinnoh, there
are said to be mirage Pokémon.\f
If we can obtain data on them,
it may shed some light on how the\r
process of Pokémon evolution works.\f
I need help from each one of you
on this grand undertaking.\f
Help me find these Pokémon that are
considered to be mirages.\f
DP.052.0007:
[0103,0000]: What are you saying?
I didn’t get no Pokédex!\f
DP.052.0008:
Rowan: ...And who was it that bolted
from the lab before I had a chance\r
to give you a Pokédex?\f
But that’s water under the bridge!\f
Seeing Pokémon with your own eyes
is important for you in becoming\r
a better Trainer, too.\f
DP.052.0009:
Dawn: You can count on me!
It will help with my Pokédex, too.\f
[0103,0000], you’ll go too,
won’t you?\f
DP.052.0010:
Lucas: You bet, Professor!\f
This will add to the Pokédex
database and help advance your\r
research.\f
DP.052.0011:
Rowan: Hm! I would have made all of
you go regardless... Anyway.\f
Now, there are three lakes.
Fortuitously, there are three of you.\f
You should therefore split up and
investigate the lakes individually.\f
DP.052.0012: Rowan: Dawn, you go to Lake Verity.\f
DP.052.0014: Rowan: Lucas, you go to Lake Verity.\f
DP.052.0016:
Rowan: [0103,0000], I want you to
investigate Lake Acuity.\f
That would take you almost to
Snowpoint City.\f
The road there is harsh. I need
a tough Trainer to go.\f
DP.052.0017:
[0103,0000]: Uh... Yeah, of course!
Gramps, you know how to call it!\r
You can tell I’m the hotness!\f
DP.052.0018:
Rowan: And that leaves [0103,0000]
with Lake Valor.\f
The lake is between Veilstone
and Pastoria, if memory serves...\f
DP.052.0019:
Rowan: ...
...Has it stopped?\f
Are all of you unharmed?\f
Let’s go outside, but be careful.
I’m concerned about the city.\f
DP.052.0020:
Rowan: [0103,0001], hurry along.
But beware of aftershocks.\f
DP.052.0021:
Every book contains someone’s
memories of various places and times.\f
Libraries aren’t just buildings where
books are kept, you know.\f
They’re where different places
and times are gathered together.
DP.052.0022:
W-was that an earthquake?
I didn’t imagine it?
DP.052.0023:
Someone’s always checked out the
book I want to read...
DP.052.0024:
I’m feeling wobbly...\f
Even though the tremor has ended,
my knees still feel like jelly...
DP.052.0025:
This book is titled “Sinnoh
Myth.” Want to read it?
DP.052.0027:
“Sinnoh Myth”\f
Betray not your anger, lest ??? will
come.\f
Weep not with sorrow, or ??? will
draw near.\f
When joy and enjoyment come natural
as the very air, that is happiness.\f
Let such be blessed by the hand
of Master ???.\f
Those words were spoken often
as customary.
DP.052.0028:
This book is titled “Sinnoh Region’s
Mythology.” Want to read it?
DP.052.0030:
“Sinnoh Region’s Mythology”\f
Long ago, when Sinnoh had just been
made, Pokémon and humans led\r
separate lives.\f
That is not to say they did not help
each other. No, indeed they did.\f
They supplied each other with goods,
and supported each other.\f
A Pokémon proposed to the others
to always be ready to help humans.\f
It asked that Pokémon be ready to
appear before humans always.\f
Thus, to this day, Pokémon appear
to us if we venture into tall grass.
DP.052.0031:
This book is titled “Sinnoh’s
Myth.” Want to read it?
DP.052.0033:
“Sinnoh’s Myth”\f
Three Pokémon there were.\f
Into the lakes they dove.
Deep, deep, drawing no breath.\f
Deeper, deeper they dove.
Into suffocating depths they dove.\f
Deeper, then deepest they alight.
From the lake floor they rise.\f
Bearing with them the power to make
vast lands, they rise again.
DP.052.0034:
This book is titled “Veilstone’s
Myth.” Want to read it?
DP.052.0036:
“Veilstone’s Myth”\f
A young man, callow and foolish in
innocence, came to own a sword.\f
With it, he smote Pokémon, which gave
sustenance, with carefree abandon.\f
Those not taken as food, he
discarded, with no afterthought.\f
The following year, no Pokémon
appeared. Larders grew bare.\f
...Would you like to keep reading?
DP.052.0037:
The young man, seeking the missing
Pokémon, journeyed afar.\f
Long did he search. And far and wide,
too, until one he did find.\f
Asked he, “Why do you hide?”
To which the Pokémon replied...\f
“If you bear your sword to bring
harm upon us, with claws and\r
fangs, we will exact a toll.\f
“From your kind we will take our
toll, for it must be done.\f
“Done it must be to guard ourselves
and for it, I apologize.”\f
...Would you like to keep reading?
DP.052.0038:
To the skies, the young man shouted
his dismay.\f
“In having found the sword, I have
lost so much.\f
“Gorged with power, I grew blind
to Pokémon being alive.\f
“I will never fall savage again.
This sword I denounce and forsake.\f
“I plead for forgiveness,
for I was but a fool.”\f
So saying, the young man hurled the
sword to the ground, snapping it.\f
Seeing this, the Pokémon disappeared
to a place beyond seeing...
DP.052.0039:
This ancient book is titled “The
Original Story.” Want to read it?
DP.052.0041:
In the beginning, there was only
a churning turmoil of chaos.\f
At the heart of chaos, where all
things became one, appeared an Egg.\f
Having tumbled from the vortex, the
Egg gave rise to the Original One.\f
...Would you like to keep reading?
DP.052.0042:
From itself, two beings the Original
One did make.\f
Time started to spin.
Space began to expand.\f
From itself again, three living things
the Original One did make.\f
...Would you like to keep reading?
DP.052.0043:
The two beings wished, and from them,
matter came to be.\f
The three living things wished, and
from them, spirit came to be.\f
The world created, the Original One
took to unyielding sleep...
DP.052.0044:
This book is titled “A Horrific
Myth.” Want to read it?
DP.052.0046:
Look not into the Pokémon’s eyes.\f
In but an instant, you’ll have no
recollection of who you are.\f
Return home, but how?
When there is nothing to remember?\f
Dare not touch the Pokémon’s body.\f
In but three short days, all emotions
will drain away.\f
Above all, above all, harm not
the Pokémon.\f
In a scant five days, the offender
will grow immobile in entirety.
DP.052.0047:
This book is titled “Sinnoh Folk
Stories.”\f
DP.052.0048: Which story would you like to read?
DP.052.0050:
“Sinnoh Folk Story 1”\f
Pick clean the bones of Pokémon
caught in the sea or stream.\f
Thank them for the meals they
provide, and pick their bones clean.\f
When the bones are as clean as can
be, set them free in the water from\r
which they came.\f
The Pokémon will return, fully
fleshed, and it begins anew.
DP.052.0051:
“Sinnoh Folk Story 2”\f
There lived a Pokémon in a forest.\f
In the forest, the Pokémon shed its
hide to sleep as a human.\f
Awakened, the human dons the
Pokémon hide to roam villages.
DP.052.0052:
“Sinnoh Folk Story 3”\f
There once were Pokémon that
became very close to humans.\f
There once were humans and Pokémon
that ate together at the same table.\f
It was a time when there existed no
differences to distinguish the two.
DP.052.0053:
On this floor, you will find many
books on the myths and legends of\r
the Sinnoh region.
